purpose: Every external integration: what it is, who owns the client, what it is authoritative for, how it fails, and what it can never be asked.
provenance: AST census of literal requests.* sites + subprocess sites + try/except ImportError sites, cross-read against Cycles 1-4.

external_services.idempotency_key: *** NONE. The backend has no idempotency key. *** This single fact is why PostState.needs_reconcile exists.
external_services.critical_shape: *** _postiz_permalink ALWAYS returns None BY DESIGN. A fresh Postiz publish CANNOT self-promote to `published` — it lands `submitted` with no URL and parks in needs_reconcile. The steady-state happy path is submitting -> submitted -> needs_reconcile -> published. ***
external_services.status_endpoint_shape: NO per-post endpoint. An unknown id is a row ABSENT from a 200-OK LIST page -> {'status':'unknown'} -> DOES NOT RAISE. This is the ONLY reason the terminal ladder works at all.
external_services.lifecycle: postiz_lifecycle.ensure_up SHELLS DOCKER from publish_due when `due` is non-empty
external_services.known_operational_hazard: the Mastra 1600-column pg crash-loop recurs on Postiz upgrades (project memory)

external_services.sibling_parity: VERIFIED byte-for-byte symmetric with Postiz on the publish failure ladder. The parity HOLDS.
external_services.status_endpoint_shape: *** HAS a true per-post lookup. An unknown id is a 404 -> RAISES RuntimeError. *** THE DIVERGENCE IS IN THE REMOTE API, NOT IN FANOPS CODE — both clients raise identically on >=300 (metrics.py:164-165 vs :516-517). Cycle 3 mis-attributed this to a FanOps sibling-parity divergence; Cycle 4 corrected it.
external_services.consequence: *** The reconcile terminal ladder sits on the SUCCESSFUL-POLL branch, so a raising poll can never reach it. The escalation and give-up are DEAD CODE ON ZERNIO, and the `gave_up` digest bucket NEVER FIRES for TikTok. C3-F2. ***

external_services.authoritative_for: *** INSTAGRAM PERFORMANCE METRICS — the SOLE IG metric reader. *** Also hashtag reach + co-occurrence discovery.
external_services.NOT_authoritative_for: PUBLISHING. Publishing goes via Postiz ONLY. (Project memory: fanops-account-connection-truth.)
external_services.client_shape: an INJECTABLE `get` so tests never touch the network — which is why an AST pass over literal requests.* calls does NOT see it.
external_services.budget: 30/7-day query budget, persisted in hashtag_budget.json under a flock
external_services.known_constraint: IG has NO trending-by-topic endpoint, so co-occurrence harvesting off top_media captions is the Graph-native discovery path.
external_services.known_operational_hazard: *** all 3 IG accounts share ONE Meta credential (ig_user_id=None), so the independent publish-verification check is BLIND to 2 of 3. MOL-112. ***

external_services.authoritative_for: public media hosting for the publish path
external_services.idempotency: *** TRUE. The object key is fanops/{sha256(file)[:32]}{suffix} — CONTENT-ADDRESSED, so a re-upload overwrites the same object. ***

external_services.transport: *** A SUBPROCESS (`claude -p`), NOT AN HTTP API. *** It rides the operator's existing `claude login`.
external_services.critical_fact: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is NOT required, and `claude --bare -p` PROVABLY FAILS because it never reads the keychain (llm.py:12-23).
external_services.authoritative_for: answering the four agent-gate kinds (moments, moment_hooks, captions; intro_match is unanswerable)

external_services.critical_constraint: *** ffmpeg selects its MUXER BY FILE EXTENSION. A temp file must be <dst>.part.mp4, not <dst>.part (COUP-07 / MOL-78). ***

external_services.known_incident: stale ASR pins in a live .env (override=True beats everything) produced nonsense subtitles; provenance is now logged.

external_services.optionality: *** THE ONLY FAIL-CLOSED OPTIONAL DEPENDENCY IN THE SYSTEM. *** With smart_framing ON (the DEFAULT) and cv2 absent, framing.require_cv2 raises ToolchainMissingError -> EXIT 2. Every other extra fails OPEN. INV-17.

external_services.authoritative_for: *** ALL SCHEDULING. There is no cron, no APScheduler, no in-process timer. ***
agents: com.fanops.run (RunAtLoad + KeepAlive; interval rides EnvironmentVariables.FANOPS_DAEMON_INTERVAL — there is NO StartInterval)
external_services.critical_constraint: *** the plist BAKES A FULL PATH at install time, because launchd supplies a bare one. A stale baked PATH pinned an old `claude` binary and killed every gate for 3 days (project memory: daemon-only-failures-check-plist-path). ***

external_services.note: SHELLED FROM THE PUBLISH PATH — publish_due calls postiz_lifecycle.ensure_up when `due` is non-empty.

external_services.contract: *** READS fail OPEN; WRITES fail CLOSED. *** set_secret writes, then READS IT BACK, and raises OSError if the value does not round-trip — LOAD-BEARING, because the caller then scrubs the plaintext .env fallback on success. An accepted-but-dropped write would erase the secret from BOTH stores.

http_library: requests — the SOLE HTTP library in src/fanops (AST-verified: 15 literal requests.* sites, no aiohttp/httpx/urllib3-direct).
integration_invariants: NO network call and NO heavy subprocess EVER runs inside the ledger lock (L1). Verified at every site.
_publish_one (run.py:296) is the SOLE caller of poster.publish. INV-09.
NEITHER publish backend has an idempotency key. Every ambiguous send PARKS in needs_reconcile and is NEVER re-POSTed. This is the entire double-publish defence.
Meta Graph is a METRIC READER ONLY. It never publishes.
